free_text: Xenon yields in the fission of heavy elements by
medium-energy protons.
beam current was 0.5-1 uA.
the time of irradiation was from 5-25 minutes.
3 in. x 3.in. NaI crystal. the efficiency of
the spectrometer was determined with a calibrated gas
phase beta-proportional counter.
Uncertainty was compounded from estimated
errors in the beam monitoring, in the measurement of
activities and chemical yields, and in analysis of
gamma-ray spectra and decay curves.
V.Mclane's remark for O0727.027 has been
included.
BIB section upgraded. Correction in
author' list has been done, according V.Zerkin remarks.
Correction in Subents 002-028, according new Exfor
rules.
SD: Corrections in all Subents. Subents
004, 007, 010, 012 deleted (arithmetic cr.sect. ratio
from the data presented in other Subents).
ERR-T -> DATA-ERR.
The target foil of 232Th and the copper monitor foil
had surface densities of 80.8, and 45.9 mg/cm2,
respectively. A target was assembled by clamping in
an aluminium holder a copper monitor foil and a heavy
metal foil held in an envelope of aluminium foil
(5.5 mg/cm2). The foil was pressed between aluminium
plates in a vise and then carefully sheared to ensure
edge alignment.
the cross sections are uncorrected for the
growth resulting from decay of I-135.
Table II from Canad.J.Chem.,48(1970)1924
SD: SF4=Xe-135g -> Xe-135; SF5=IND/M+
deleted from REACTION code. SAMPLE added. STATUS
updated. ERR-ANALYS moved to Subent 001.

233U,the target material was finely powdered uraniunl
oxide (5-12 mg) wrapped in aluminium foil of surface
density 5.5 mg/cm2. A target was assembled by
clamping in an aluminium holder a copper monitor foil
and a heavy metal foil held in an envelope of
aluminium foil (5.5 mg/cm2). The foil was pressed
between aluminium plates in a vise and then carefully
sheared to ensure edge alignment.
